Headline: Develop an AI Agent for InEight
Problem Statement: Project teams must navigate multiple applications, reports, and training resources to find answers or understand next steps, creating friction, delays, and inconsistent use of the platform. Knowledge exists, but it is fragmented and not accessible in the moment decisions are made.
Expected Outcome: Quickly interact with all the material inside of InEight in context of the solution that is being used.  This includes training material and reporting.  These agents will support the overall Kiewit agent.
Possible Solution: Build an AI Agent that is available in all applications for InEight
ROI Description: Kiewit field personal is 7,300 FTE's.  50% of these FTE's interact with the system on a daily basis.  By providing training and reporting in real time effiency can be improved by at least 5%

Headline: Document Control Automation
Problem Statement: Critical project information is locked in documents that require manual search, expert interpretation, and repeated handoffs, slowing response times and increasing dependency on a small number of specialists for routine questions and actions.
Expected Outcome: Enable conversational interaction with project documents to get answers more quickly and automate document-related workflows. Expands the footprint of people who are able to get work done without having to make requests to SMEs or specialists
Possible Solution: Develop an AI Agent, accessed through a new web and mobile UI, that uses document intelligence to interact with the project documents uploaded into InEight Document.  The Agent can answer questions, anticipate issues based on document content, and initiate actions inside the system (e.g., send an RFI and include this content)
ROI Description: Kiewit has 500 FTE's and partners in the system on a daily basis.  By building automated form generation of RFI's, submittals, and transmittals this process can be significantly enhanced by at least 25%

Headline: Estimate Takeoff Automation
Problem Statement: Quantity takeoff is a labor-intensive, manual process that varies by estimator and project, creating schedule delays, cost variability, and limited scalability during peak workload periods.
Expected Outcome: Opportunity to create a Takeoff Shared Service and automate the takeoff process through the Model and 2D drawings
Possible Solution: Opportunity to create a Takeoff Shared Service and automate the takeoff process through the Model and 2D drawings
ROI Description: Kiewit has 750 FTE's that participate in quantity takeoff on a daily basis automation of 50% of this time

Headline: Estimate Conformance
Problem Statement: Estimate conformance requires repetitive manual checks, rollups, and normalization that consume expert time and introduce inconsistency, slowing downstream activities such as scheduling and risk analysis.
Expected Outcome: Dramatically shorten estimate conformance timelines using AI to learn from prior conformance routines to auto-complete actions such as roll-ups and splits, account code assignments, resource normalization, and other time consuming tasks
Possible Solution: Estimate conformance could be automated through standard account codes and business line structure
ROI Description: Kiewit has 25 FTE's that participate in the Estimate conformance process on a daily basis the task can be reduced by 80%

Headline: Project Schedule Creation
Problem Statement: Initial project schedules take significant time to produce and depend heavily on individual experience, resulting in inconsistent structure, increased risk, and delayed visibility into execution feasibility.
Expected Outcome: Dramatically reduce time required to create preliminary construction schedules by intelligently leveraging information in the current estimate and historical schedules to auto create WBS/activities, durations, logic relationships, and more
Possible Solution: From the estimate or estimate conformance a schedule could be generated based on past projects and standard account codes
ROI Description: Kiewit has 100 people that participate in schedule creation on a daily basis while reducing risk and creating automated schedules reduces this time by 50%

Headline: Project Risk Creation and Monitoring
Problem Statement: Project risks are often identified late and inconsistently, relying on manual judgment rather than systematic use of historical patterns, leading to reactive mitigation and reduced predictability.
Expected Outcome: Identify risks earlier in the project lifecycle by  intelligently leveraging information in the current estimate and historical schedules to auto create a risk register
Possible Solution: At estimate time a risk register could be generated based on standard account codes and applied against the generated estimate and schedule.
ROI Description: The ability to automate a risk register for a project based on previous work is significant

Headline: Project Quantity Overrun Tracking
Problem Statement: Quantity overruns are typically detected after cost impacts occur, due to limited ability to continuously compare planned and actual quantities against historical benchmarks and emerging trends.
Expected Outcome: Create more accurate quantity expectations for projects by continually learning from previous projects and tracking where quantity busts are most likely to occur
Possible Solution: Takeoffs would be automatically compared and stored  in Design for future use
ROI Description: Having the ability review and automate quantity overrun projects for an estimate will have significant value

Headline: Work Package Creation
Problem Statement: Work package development requires substantial manual effort to assemble plans, scope, and sequencing, creating delays and variability in quality while consuming significant field and engineering capacity.
Expected Outcome: A shared service could complete the initial plans based on project needs through automation
Possible Solution: A shared service could complete the initial plans based on project needs through automation
ROI Description: Kiewit has 2500 FTE's that spend 20% generating work plans on a daily basis by improving the creation and quality of workplaning this process can improved by 50%

Headline: Compliance Form Creation
Problem Statement: Safety and compliance forms are manually created and customized for each work plan, consuming time, introducing errors, and increasing the risk of inconsistent or incomplete documentation.
Expected Outcome: Eliminate manual effort required to create safety forms associated with an upcoming work plan
Possible Solution: InEight needs to automate the creation of Compliance forms for Web and mobile based work plans b asked on past patterns
ROI Description: Kiewit has 50 FTE's developing forms on a daily basis by automating this process we can improve safety and efficiency

Headline: Completion Form Creation
Problem Statement: Quality checklists and completion documentation are manually generated and maintained, creating administrative overhead and increasing the likelihood of missed or inconsistent quality controls.
Expected Outcome: Eliminate manual effort required to create quality checklists associated with an upcoming work plan
Possible Solution: InEight needs to automate the creation of Completion forms for Web and mobile based on past patterns, types of work, components, and plans
ROI Description: Kiewit has 50 FTE's developing forms on a daily basis by automating this process we can improve quality and efficiency

Headline: Short Interval Planning  Creation
Problem Statement: Short interval planning depends on manual translation of schedules into near-term execution steps, making it time-consuming, error-prone, and difficult to keep aligned with current project conditions.
Expected Outcome: Save time by auto creating short interval planning steps aligned to current schedules and work plans
Possible Solution: Based on previous projects schedule activities, and work plans an automated short interval plan could be generated
ROI Description: Kiewit has 500 FTE's that participate in the process on a daily basis if we can automate 3 week scheduling based on workplans we can improve schedule accuracy and reduce time spent in the field
